title: Quercus ciliaris
intro: Quercus ciliaris, commonly known as the Indian evergreen oak or Himalayan oak, is a species of deciduous tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The health benefits of Quercus ciliaris include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have potential applications in treating various diseases such as cardiovascular disorders and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of Quercus ciliaris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Quercus ciliaris is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Quercus ciliaris is a species of oak tree that belongs to the beech family, Fagaceae. It is classified as a deciduous tree, native to the tropical regions of Asia. Within the genus Quercus, it is placed in the subgenus Cerris, which contains species of oak trees commonly found in warm and temperate climates. The classification of Quercus ciliaris is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Tracheophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Fagales, family Fagaceae, genus Quercus, subgenus Cerris, and species Q. ciliaris. Quercus ciliaris is also known by its other name, Cyclobalanopsis ciliaris.
uses: Quercus ciliaris, commonly known as the Sessile Oak, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's bark, leaves, and acorns are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the bark is used to treat fever, while the leaves are used to treat various skin conditions. The acorns are believed to have antioxidant properties and are used to treat digestive issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these medicinal uses.
benefits: Quercus ciliaris, also known as the southern red oak, has been found to have potential health benefits. The plant's leaves are rich in antioxidants, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Research suggests that extracts from Quercus ciliaris may also have antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating infections and wounds. Additionally, the plant's bark has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. Further studies are needed to fully understand the extent of Quercus ciliaris' health benefits.
properties: Quercus ciliaris, also known as the bladder nut oak, is a species with reported traditional medicinal uses. Its therapeutic properties are believed to include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Studies have suggested that extracts from the plant may have potential in treating various health issues, including cancer and cardiovascular diseases. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Quercus ciliaris for medicinal purposes. However, its potential uses make it an area of interest for future scientific investigation.

parts: Quercus ciliaris is a species of oak tree native to the Himalayas and the Indian subcontinent, and its bark, leaves, and roots are considered medicinal. The bark of Quercus ciliaris is used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. The leaves of the tree are used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions and as a diuretic. The roots of Quercus ciliaris are used to treat digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The medicinal properties of Quercus ciliaris are still being studied and used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
